page: Amazon Web Services m6a.large is a general-purpose virtual server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the physical single-core limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 141.24 GB/sec and uncached large block read/write speeds at 31.17 GB/sec and 21.04 GB/sec respectively.

Real-world workload simulations show average performance for single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation with Clang score in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Text processing and small-model LLM text generation perform at average levels, while image processing and static web serving throughput are weaker.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation in an entry-level general-purpose tier. It is best suited for lightweight workloads that benefit from strong single-core capabilities but do not require high memory bandwidth or multi-threaded parallel processing. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale development environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.large is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 2 vCPUs from 1 physical core, 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into lower percentiles. Single-threaded compression and text processing perform at average levels. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level general-purpose workloads, making it suitable for basic web serving, lightweight text processing, and small-scale development environments that do not require intensive multi-threaded processing or high memory throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.large is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, suitable for basic web serving and development tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.large is a general-purpose server with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for lightweight single-core tasks.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual server offering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for entry-level workloads.

Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage
Average performance in single-threaded compression and text processing
